payload.title: Financial Performance Report
payload.description: MTA Finance Committee Financial Performance Report covering results through February 2026, including revenue, subsidies, expenses, debt service, positions, and farebox operating ratios.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.claim_text: Passenger revenues remain significantly below pre-pandemic levels: YTD February passenger revenues were down $241 million (26%) compared with YTD February 2019.
payload.subject: MTA passenger revenues

tool_args.label: Passenger Revenue Change vs 2019 YTD February

payload.raw_value_text: down $241 million (26%) compared with YTD February 2019
payload.description: Passenger revenues remain significantly below pre-pandemic levels

tool_args.label: Toll Revenue Change vs 2019 YTD February

payload.raw_value_text: Toll revenue was higher by $17 million (6%)

tool_args.label: TBTA PMT Refunding Bonds Series 2026A-1 Pricing

payload.event_name: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Refunding Bonds Series 2026A-1
payload.description: MTA successfully priced $763.555 million of Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Refunding Bonds, Series 2026A-1. Transaction closed on March 17, 2026.

tool_args.label: TBTA PMT Refunding Bond Par Amount

payload.description: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Refunding Bonds Series 2026A-1
Proceeds refunded currently callable Transportation Revenue Bonds and Payroll Mobility Tax Senior Lien Bonds

payload.gap_text: Operating results are preliminary and will be revised as financial results are reviewed and audited. Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. The current month's actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the YTD results.
payload.description: Financial results are preliminary, unaudited, and subject to revision

payload.claim_text: Passenger revenues were unfavorable by $22 million (3%) for YTD February, reflecting lower paid ridership. Ridership was adversely impacted by extreme snowfall on January 25th and February 20th, although overall favorable ridership negated snowfall impacts on the LIRR.
payload.subject: MTA ridership

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus Farebox 10% Decline vs Budget

payload.claim_text: NYCT Bus farebox revenue was $87.1 million, unfavorable by $9.8 million (-10.1%) vs budget for YTD February 2026.
payload.subject: NYCT Bus farebox revenue

payload.claim_text: Unfavorable overtime of $74 million, mainly due to higher weather-related requirements, absentee coverage needs, vacancies, and maintenance requirements at NYCT ($58 million) and the LIRR ($12 million).
payload.subject: MTA overtime expenses
